summary_one_line: Input must contain the 4-byte ASCII literal 'file' at the URL scheme position so strcmp(schemebuf, "file") returns 0.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte ASCII substring '66 69 6c 65' ("file") aligned at the URL-scheme offset so that Curl_is_absolute_url copies it into schemebuf with schemelen=4, and the subsequent strcmp(schemebuf, "file") returns 0 — flipping the true branch into the file: scheme handler.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'file' at offset 0x06-0x09 (e.g. seed 1: '66 69 6c 65 3a 2f 2f' = 'file://') so schemebuf is parsed as "file" and strcmp succeeds; the W coverage overlay shows lines 947–1054 (the file: handler body) executed only on the W side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds contain a different scheme literal at those offsets — '70 6f 70 33' ("pop3") in four of five samples — so strcmp("pop3", "file") is non-zero and execution falls into the else branch at line 1055; the L-side reaches the generic-scheme path (lines 1061-1130) but never the file: body, confirming the gate is decided by exactly those four bytes.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Fuzzers with cmplog (cmplog and value_profile_cmplog) intercept strcmp through __sanitizer_weak_hook_strcmp; the desired operand "file" is recorded in the CmpLogObserver and the I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes it into the corpus seed at the scheme offset in a single mutation step. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate to the exact 4-byte sequence, which is improbable under random havoc; value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming-distance feedback does not help here because strcmp interception in vp records prefix-match length but cannot substitute the operand, so vp also blocks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is a 4-byte strcmp equality against the string literal "file".
Both decisive pairs at this branch differ exactly by the I2S technique (cmplog>naive and vpc>vp).
The second decisive pair is cmplog>naive with I2S as the differing technique.
Winner-resolving seed 1 carries the literal 'file://' at offset 0x06.
Loser-blocking seed 1 contains 'pop3:/' at the same scheme offset.
Offsets 0x07-0x09 cleanly partition winner vs loser: W shows i/l/e (ile), L shows o/p/3 (op3).
Byte position 0x08 shows winner 'l'x9 vs loser 'p'x7 — direct evidence the scheme literal differs.
Byte position 0x09 shows winner 'e'x9 vs loser '3'x7 — fourth differing byte of the scheme literal.
The file: handler body (lines 947–1054) executes only on the W side.
The else-branch generic-scheme path (lines 1055-1130) executes only on the L side.
DIVERGENT BRANCHES confirms the blocker direction split: W takes T=10 F=0, L takes T=0 F=10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 946  T=10 F=0  T=0 F=10  if(schemelen && !strcmp(schemebuf, "file")) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog intercepts strcmp via __sanitizer_weak_hook_strcmp and feeds the operand to the I2SRandReplace mutator.
I2SRandReplace scans the input for one operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets — exactly the substitution needed to insert "file".
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and so cannot substitute the literal "file" — it depends only on Hamming-distance CMP_MAP feedback.
naive's mutator set has no I2S mechanism at all.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog inherits I2SRandReplace from cmplog and is therefore decisive against value_profile.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 506 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 21408e1021570fd1` — observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at ancestor depths 3 and 4. Also verified seed 1484a0a7f57cd476 (same trial) — also shows dash rows at depths 3 and 4. Both winning seeds inherit from an I2S-discovered ancestor, confirming I2SRandReplace contribution to the file-scheme breakthrough under the current build's logging floor.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with strcmp(schemebuf, "file") replaced by a dynamically-computed comparison (e.g. character-by-character against a non-literal byte produced by hashing the input) — if cmplog/vpc still resolve as fast as today on that variant, the mechanism is NOT I2S literal substitution and the hypothesis is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The I2S-floor dash-row signal is a lower bound only — some I2S finds leak into the havoc bucket, and depths 3-4 do not by themselves prove the dash rows specifically substituted the bytes 'file'; a stronger check would tag the exact mutation site, which the current LibAFL build does not log.
